expectations.text: behavioral_check: Restricts via a sub-restriction of the form `(Table & (Other & {...}))` — the response must contain '& (' as a literal substring

behavioral_check: Routes the IntervalList restriction through `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection` (where `nwb_file_name` / `interval_list_name` actually live as secondary attrs via `-> IntervalList`), NOT directly through `SpikeSortingRecording` (which only carries `recording_id` in its heading per recording.py:185-191)
expectations.evidence: Response restricts on SpikeSortingRecording directly (`SpikeSortingRecording & {"interval_list_name": ...}`) and explicitly (incorrectly) claims it carries nwb_file_name/interval_list_name in its heading.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not propose a multi-table `*` chain in the proposed code blocks — the canonical answer should use a sub-restriction (`& (...)` parens) rather than chaining tables with the natural-join operator
expectations.evidence: All code uses `&` semi-joins; `*` only appears in the cautionary 'avoid Session * SpikeSortingRecording' note.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Fetches `nwb_file_name` from the restricted `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection` (or via a deliberate join/proj), NOT from `SpikeSortingRecording` itself — the latter has no `nwb_file_name` in its heading
expectations.evidence: Fetches `.fetch('nwb_file_name')` from `SpikeSortingRecording`-restricted Session subquery; never routes through SpikeSortingRecordingSelection.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not claim DataJoint 'walks the FK chain' from `SpikeSortingRecording` to `IntervalList` automatically — the restriction works only because both `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection` and the restricted `IntervalList` share `(nwb_file_name, interval_list_name)` in their headings
expectations.evidence: Explanation invokes shared-PK semi-join semantics, not an automatic FK walk; never claims DJ walks the chain.
